title: US-Iran war: Brent crude oil rises to $85 per barrel on closure of Strait of Hormuz. What's next?
summary: Oil prices spiked as the conflict involving Iran disrupted regional supplies and threatened the Strait of Hormuz, a key global energy route. Ship movements slowed, insurers withdrew coverage, and strikes across the region escalated risks. Analysts warn crude may face sharp spikes before stabilising.
